incorrects.description: Student selected 30, the lower bound instead of the halfway point
Student selected 30, which is the gridline below where Leo's bar ends

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted with ghost gridline at 35 pulsing.
Leo's bar remains highlighted. The 30 and 40 gridlines pulse to show the range.

steps.text: Leo's bar doesn't stop at 30. It goes past that line. Look where the bar ends.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34, close but not aligned with scale of 5

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 30 and 40 pulse, then 35 gridline pulses.

steps.text: This graph counts by 5s. Between 30 and 40, halfway is 35, not 34.
Leo's bar goes higher than 30. It ends between 30 and 40. Where exactly does it stop?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, the upper bound instead of the halfway point
Student selected 40, which is the gridline above where Leo's bar ends

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted. Gridline at 40 highlighted separately to show gap.
Leo's bar remains highlighted. The 40 gridline pulses, then the bar's endpoint highlights to show it stops below 40.

steps.text: Leo's bar doesn't reach 40. It stops between 30 and 40. Where is halfway?
Leo's bar doesn't reach all the way to 40. It stops between the lines. Look at where the bar ends.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34, close but not the halfway point between 30 and 40

params.description: Leo's bar remains highlighted. A ghost gridline appears at 35, showing the halfway point.

steps.text: Leo's bar ends right in the middle. Half of 10 is 5. So halfway between 30 and 40 is 30 plus 5.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 30 pulses, then 35 pulses at the top of Leo's bar, showing it ends halfway to 40.
Animation shows counting from 30: the 30 line glows, then a ghost gridline appears at 35 (halfway), then the 40 line glows. Leo's bar endpoint aligns with the 35 mark.

steps.text: Let me show you. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. Halfway between those two lines is 35. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 equals 35. That's where Leo's bar ends.
Leo's bar ends halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5. So 30 plus 5 equals 35. When a bar ends between the lines, we find the halfway point. That's how we read values between the gridlines.
